banner: Week 1 · The Rhythm, the Work, the Goals
essential_question: What's my job, what's your job, and where are we headed?
objective: Explain the Concept–Build–Prove rhythm and how the independent Math-U-See work fits, and set your goals for the year.

content.prompt: You've met the tools and made your first measurement. What surprised you? What are you already curious to build?

content.heading: Concept → Build → Prove
bullets: Concept: I teach the idea; you explore it (read the concept note first).
Build: you make it — with your hands, in CAD, or by measuring.
Prove: you reason it out, we review your Math-U-See work, and you take a short checkpoint.
Between sessions: read the note, then do the Math-U-See Lesson Practice + Test on your own.

content.heading: You move on when you own it
content.note: Each lesson's mastery comes from your MUS Lesson Test + the session checkpoint + your build. Emerging → Proficient → Mastered. We don't race; we make sure it's real. Unit Tests come after Lessons 10, 19, and 30, with a Final at the end.

content.preview: Next week, Lesson 1: Points, Lines, Rays & Segments — read the concept note first.

success_criteria.text: I can describe what happens in a Concept, Build, and Prove session
I can explain how my independent Math-U-See work fits the week

questions.prompt: In one sentence each, describe what you do in a Concept, a Build, and a Prove session.
questions.answer: Concept: learn/explore the idea; Build: make it (hands/CAD/measure); Prove: reason it out, review MUS, checkpoint.
questions.explain: Full credit: all three sessions described distinctly.

questions.prompt: Write two goals for the year — one about a skill, one about something you want to build.
questions.answer: Open — one skill goal + one build goal.
questions.explain: Rubric: two goals, at least one naming a concrete build/project.

questions.prompt: Close: what's the one part of geometry you're most looking forward to?
